steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The figure is a right triangle (indicated by the right angle marker). The two legs are 4 and $b$, and the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle, which is always the longest side) is 19.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The Pythagorean theorem states that for a right triangle with legs $a$ and $b$ and hypotenuse $c$: $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$. Substituting: $4^2 + b^2 = 19^2$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Check: $4^2 = 16$, $19^2 = 361$, so $b^2 = 361 - 16 = 345$, meaning $b = \sqrt{345}$. This is valid for a right triangle.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• $4b=19$ $4b=19$ treats the relationship as a product, which has no geometric basis here. Eliminated.
  • $4+b=19$ $4+b=19$ treats the relationship as a sum of sides, which is not the Pythagorean theorem. Eliminated.
  • ${4}^{2}+{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$ ${4}^{2}+{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$ is the Pythagorean theorem with legs 4 and $b$ and hypotenuse 19. That is the correct answer.
  • ${4}^{2}-{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$ ${4}^{2}-{b}^{2}={19}^{2}$ rearranges incorrectly and would make $b^2$ negative. Eliminated.
